roots-01.essence: The low place was raising you, not burying you.
roots-01.bridge: Whatever pit the seeker names — heartbreak, failure, a flat gray month — this card reframes the bottom as the place that grew them, and the proof is that they climbed out far enough to ask.
roots-01.seed: Sit flat on the playa floor after midnight, palms in the dust, and name out loud three things your worst year taught you before you stand up.

roots-02.essence: Grief is love that kept the room.
roots-02.bridge: Whatever the seeker says is wrong with them — the tired body, the flat mood, the ache that won't schedule itself — this card asks who they are still carrying, because the body files loss under symptoms.
roots-02.seed: Carry a small stone in your pocket for one full night as someone you lost; at dawn, set it down somewhere beautiful and walk away without looking back.

roots-03.essence: What you won't let sink is pulling you under.
roots-03.bridge: Whatever the seeker is white-knuckling — a plan, a person, a version of themselves that used to work — this card names it as the weight, not the raft.
roots-03.seed: Tell one campmate the plan you've been forcing all week, then spend the whole night with no plan at all — follow whatever light pulls you.

roots-04.essence: The terrain you're searching for is behind your own eyes.
roots-04.bridge: When the seeker keeps asking what everyone else thinks — boss, lover, crowd — this card turns them around: the map isn't out there in the noise, and they've been reading other people's instead of their own.
roots-04.seed: Sit at the edge of any sound camp tonight, close your eyes for twenty slow breaths, and afterward say one true sentence about what you saw in the dark.

roots-05.essence: Let the place mark you before you try to master it.
roots-05.bridge: Whatever the seeker is trying to do perfectly on the first try — a new job, a new love, a new life — this card says get dirty first; nobody learns ground they refuse to touch.
roots-05.seed: Find the dustiest veteran you can and ask them to name one thing you're doing wrong out here. Thank them without defending yourself once.

roots-06.essence: Boundaries spoken clearly are what make surrender safe.
roots-06.bridge: When the seeker's trouble is trust — betrayed, guarded, tired of playing defense — this card says the fix is neither dropping the shell nor sealing it, but saying out loud exactly where the edges are.
roots-06.seed: Ask a campmate for exactly ten minutes of the care you actually want — a shoulder rub, silence, being listened to — and state one clear boundary before you begin.

roots-07.essence: The hard passage is not meant to be walked alone.
roots-07.bridge: Whatever hard stretch the seeker is gritting through in private — panic, doubt, a night that got too big — this card says the difficulty isn't the failure; hiding it from every other hand is.
roots-07.seed: Tell one person tonight, plainly, 'I'm having a hard time,' and let them respond without you rushing in to fix the moment for them.

roots-08.essence: Get lost on purpose; you always find your way back.
roots-08.bridge: When the seeker is over-planned and still stuck — backup plans, advice from everyone, no motion — this card prescribes the opposite: drop the map and find out who shows up when nobody's watching.
roots-08.seed: Stand at a corner you don't know and flip a coin at every intersection for thirty minutes. Let the city choose your night, then find your own way home.

roots-09.essence: What burned down can return, stranger and brighter.
roots-09.bridge: Whatever the seeker lost — the job, the marriage, the old self they keep eulogizing — this card refuses the funeral; what burned is raw material, and the rebuild carries its soul forward.
roots-09.seed: Find a burn barrel tonight, feed it one small scrap, and tell whoever is warming their hands what you're rebuilding from this year's ashes.

roots-10.essence: Dry ground greens slowly; keep bringing the water.
roots-10.bridge: Whatever the seeker let go dry — a friendship, a body, a craft — this card says it isn't dead, it's dormant, and the cure is small water on a schedule, not one heroic flood.
roots-10.seed: Carry extra water tonight and offer it to anyone who's run dry — no speech, no lesson, just the pour.

roots-11.essence: You are a body, and it has been keeping score.
roots-11.bridge: Whatever the seeker calls a thinking problem — the loop, the indecision, the 3 a.m. spiral — this card moves it down into the body, which has been asking for rest in a language they keep ignoring.
roots-11.seed: Before midnight, lie flat somewhere quiet with your boots off and both palms on your ribs, and count your own heartbeats to one hundred before you move.

roots-12.essence: The empty place is the material, not the obstacle.
roots-12.bridge: When the seeker lists what's missing — money, time, talent, the right conditions — this card flips the ledger: the lack is the assignment, and hard edges are what give the work its shape.
roots-12.seed: Draw something enormous in the dust with your heel tonight — no tools, no plan — and stand beside it until one stranger stops to look.

trunk-01.essence: Circle slowly, then commit with your whole jaw.
trunk-01.bridge: Whether the seeker is rushing everything or deciding nothing, this card holds both teeth of one skill — slow the watching way down, then make the single bite count.
trunk-01.seed: Say one unhedged sentence tonight — no 'maybe,' no 'kind of,' no laugh at the end — to the person it most concerns, and let it stand.

trunk-02.essence: The break comes just past where you'd have quit.
trunk-02.bridge: Whatever long slog the seeker is about to abandon — the degree, the recovery, the dry stretch of a marriage — this card asks for twenty more minutes, because the turn tends to arrive right after the urge to quit peaks.
trunk-02.seed: Mark the moment tonight when you'd normally head back to camp — then stay out one more hour on purpose, and notice what the extra hour hands you.

trunk-03.essence: Cracked and still beating are not in conflict.
trunk-03.bridge: Whatever the seeker thinks disqualifies them — the divorce, the relapse, the visible scar — this card counts it as proof of function: the crack is where the light gets out, and the beat never stopped.
trunk-03.seed: Show a stranger one of your cracks tonight — a scar, a failure, a bad year — told plainly, without the joke you usually armor it with.

trunk-04.essence: Mark your days out loud or they blur into one.
trunk-04.bridge: When the seeker says life has gone blurry — weeks vanish, nothing lands — this card says the problem isn't the pace, it's that nothing gets marked, and unmarked days scatter like dust.
trunk-04.seed: At the next turn of the hour, stop wherever you are, plant both feet, and say out loud the one thing from today worth keeping.

trunk-05.essence: There is always a center to walk back to.
trunk-05.bridge: Whatever direction the seeker has lost — career, faith, the point of any of it — this card doesn't hand them a map; it says stop navigating and walk toward whatever still feels like the middle.
trunk-05.seed: Sit in Center Camp's shade and write, on anything you can find, the one thing you always come back to. Keep it in your pocket for the rest of the burn.

trunk-06.essence: Not knowing is allowed; asking is a skill.
trunk-06.bridge: Whatever the seeker has been grinding at alone — the question they won't voice, the help they won't request — this card names the real obstacle: pride dressed up as self-sufficiency, cured by one asked question.
trunk-06.seed: Ask a stranger tonight for help with something you could technically handle yourself — patching a tire, finding a camp — and let them give it fully.

trunk-07.essence: Leave it cleaner than you found it, unseen.
trunk-07.bridge: When the seeker aches to matter — to be recognized, credited, remembered — this card offers the quieter ledger: the good done with no witness is the only kind that proves what you are.
trunk-07.seed: Do one invisible kindness tonight — stand up a stranger's fallen bike, refill a communal jug, re-stake a sagging tent line — and take the secret to bed with you.

trunk-08.essence: The unimpressive middle of your life still counts.
trunk-08.bridge: When the seeker apologizes for being between things — no title, no drama, nothing to report — this card crowns exactly that stretch: the middle is not a waiting room, it's the reign.
trunk-08.seed: Introduce yourself to three people tonight without mentioning your job, your projects, or your plans — and notice what's left to say.

trunk-09.essence: When you can't see, stop steering and shelter.
trunk-09.bridge: Whatever the seeker cannot control — a silent lover, a pending answer, a world that won't hold still — this card forbids navigating blind; sit down, hold the nearest hand, and let the weather finish.
trunk-09.seed: Pick the one problem you've been steering all week, tell it 'not tonight' out loud, and give the whole night to whatever is directly in front of you.

trunk-10.essence: Ask plainly for what you want; honor the no.
trunk-10.bridge: Whatever the seeker resents not getting — affection, credit, time — this card asks the rude question first: did they ever say it out loud, plainly, to the person who could actually give it?
trunk-10.seed: Be the clear one tonight: every time someone asks you for anything, give an honest yes or a kind no — nothing mumbled in between.

trunk-11.essence: Carry your own water; then you're free to give.
trunk-11.bridge: When the seeker's complaint is other people — the flaky friend, the absent partner, the world that won't provide — this card quietly hands the canteen back: the need they keep outsourcing is theirs to carry first.
trunk-11.seed: Before leaving camp tonight, pack for your own worst case — water, layers, lights — then spend the night being the one who has extra.

trunk-12.essence: Sit still; you are not behind.
trunk-12.bridge: When the seeker lists everything they're behind on, this card cancels the race — the place they're supposed to be instead of here does not exist, and one unhurried cup is the proof.
trunk-12.seed: Make the slowest drink you can manage tonight — tea, cocoa, plain warm water — and serve it to one person, taking twice as long as necessary on purpose.

branches-01.essence: Reach only as high as your roots can hold.
branches-01.bridge: Whether the seeker is dreaming big and slipping, or safe and stuck, this card checks both ends of the same tree — the reach and the root fail separately but never alone.
branches-01.seed: Tell someone tonight both halves honestly: the biggest thing you want, and the unglamorous thing that keeps you standing — without laughing either one off.

branches-02.essence: Chosen kin are real kin; name them out loud.
branches-02.bridge: When the seeker feels alone — new city, thinned friendships, a family that doesn't see them — this card points at the kin they've already half-chosen but never claimed out loud.
branches-02.seed: Tell one person at this burn, to their face, 'You're my family out here' — and watch how fast it becomes true.

branches-03.essence: Give it away and want nothing back.
branches-03.bridge: When the seeker keeps score — who called, who paid, who owes — this card tears up the ledger: give one thing clean, expecting nothing, and watch scarcity lose its grip.
branches-03.seed: Ask one stranger tonight what would make their burn easier — then quietly meet that need before sunrise, if it's in your power.

branches-04.essence: Who you are is partly what others witness.
branches-04.bridge: When the seeker is gripping an identity that stopped fitting — the title, the role, the old story — this card loosens their hands: let someone who sees them clearly say who they've become.
branches-04.seed: Gift a name tonight: watch a stranger long enough to see something true, then offer them the name it deserves — and tell them why.

branches-05.essence: You belonged before you earned anything.
branches-05.bridge: Whatever the seeker is doing to earn a place — overworking, overgiving, auditioning for love — this card calls off the tryout: belonging is offered, not achieved, and their job is to receive it.
branches-05.seed: Walk into one camp you've never visited, say it's your first time there, and receive whatever fuss they make without deflecting once.

branches-06.essence: Stop optimizing the list; become findable.
branches-06.bridge: When the seeker asks about love — where it went, why it left, whether it's coming — this card retires the shopping list and asks instead how findable they've made themselves lately.
branches-06.seed: Tell one stranger tonight a true thing about yourself that isn't on any profile — the unmarketable thing — and let the conversation go where it goes.

branches-07.essence: Luxury is a thing you give, not buy.
branches-07.bridge: Whether the seeker gives until empty or can't accept a kindness, this card works both doors — everyone deserves to be received like royalty, and everyone owes a turn in the uniform.
branches-07.seed: Set two chairs outside your camp for one hour tonight and treat every passerby like the guest of honor — directions, water, a seat, a specific compliment.

branches-08.essence: Dancing until you dissolve is a prayer with a body.
branches-08.bridge: When the seeker has gone gray and dutiful — all obligation, no appetite — this card prescribes sweat as worship: the sacred and the wild are the same fire, and they've been starving both.
branches-08.seed: Dance one full song tonight with your eyes closed, anywhere there's music, and dedicate it silently to one thing you're grateful for.

branches-09.essence: Joy needs no occasion and no permission.
branches-09.bridge: When the seeker is postponing happiness — after the deadline, after the move, after things settle — this card revokes the excuse: joy gets joined in the open, sweat and all, or not at all.
branches-09.seed: Start something small and public tonight — a chant, applause for a passing bike, a two-person parade — and count how many join before you reach fifty.

branches-10.essence: Your work tool can make useless, beautiful things.
branches-10.bridge: When the seeker's craft has gone stale — the job eating the joy that started it — this card puts the tool back in their hands as a toy: make one useless thing and remember why.
branches-10.seed: Explain the thing you're best at to a stranger tonight as if it were art, not work — what it makes, never what it earns — and watch it get interesting again.

branches-11.essence: The thing you're embarrassed to want to make is the one.
branches-11.bridge: Whatever the seeker is keeping respectable — the career, the persona, the safe answer — this card bets the good stuff lives in whatever they'd be embarrassed to want out loud.
branches-11.seed: Perform one thing tonight that's just past your edge — a bad poem to a stranger, an invented myth about the moon, a dance move that aged poorly — and don't explain it afterward.

branches-12.essence: Stop circling the edges; walk to the center.
branches-12.bridge: When the seeker has tried everything at the edges — new city, new lover, new plan — this card ends the orbit: the answer sits at the middle of the life they keep leaving, so walk in.
branches-12.seed: Walk from wherever you are straight toward the Man tonight — no stops, no detours — and stand at the exact center of the city until your question gets simpler.
